place: Troy, Waldo, Maine; South Portland, Maine
text:
Troy
A very pretty wedding was solemnized at the home of Mr. and Mrs. Guy Lenentine, South Portland, on June 20, at 7 p. m., when their daughter, Grace Christine, became the bride of Frederic J. Bagley, son of Mr. and Mrs. Fred H. Bagley of Troy. The ceremony was performed by Rev. Henry Beukleman of Camden, former pastor of the bride, using the single ring service. She was given in marriage by her father; her cousin, Miss Pauline Carmichael, acted as bridesmaid, and her brother, Hasston Lenentine, as best man. The wedding march was played by Miss Blackwell of South Portland. The bride wore a gown of white mousseline de sole with fingertip veil, and carried a shower bouquet. The bridesmaid wore orchid, in the same material, with flower wreath, and carried a bouquet of snapdragons. Following the ceremony, a delicious lunch was served, including a beautiful wedding cake which was [cut] by the bride, with the efficient assistance of the groom. Mrs. Erma Goodale Smith of Waterville and Mrs. Esther Trueworthy Reynolds of Portland, helped in serving. Among the guests were Mrs. Emma Pierce, 88 year old grandmother of the bridegroom, and Miss Pearl E. Pierce of Berlin, N. H.; Mr. and Mrs. Fred H. Bagley, Troy; Burton Lenentine, Clarence Lenentine, Miss Lillian Lenentine, Everett and Merritt Lenentine, all of Monticello; Mr. and Mrs. Lloyd Myrick of Troy; Mr. and Mrs. Kenton Bagley, Beverly, Mass.; Mr. and Mrs. Maurice Hilton, Auburn; Mr. and Mrs. Rudolph Vincent and Mr. and Mrs. Albert Bernier of Westbrook; Mr. and Mrs. Clifton Hunt, Burnham; Mrs. [---] Bagley, Unity; Mrs. Ervena Smith, Waterville; Mrs. Esther Reynolds and daughter, Virginia, Mr. and Mrs. Walter Carmichael and son Linton, Mrs. Lillian Stewart, Mrs. Desmond Frost and son, Stephen and Mr. and Mrs. Perley Dykeman, all of Portland. The 87 year old great grandmother of the bride, Mrs. Ella Danforth, of Troy, was also expected but did not feel able to make the trip. Mrs. Bagley is a graduate of Ricker Classical Institute, and Bangor School of Commerce, having been employed as stenographer for a time before her marriage. Mr. Bagley is a graduate of Unity high school and is an active Granger, being past master of Seven Star Grange. He is also past master of Star in the West Lodge of Masons at Unity, and is at present secretary of the lodge. They will live in Troy where Mr. Bagley is engaged in farming. He is also serving the R. F. D. route as carrier at this time. Both are public spirited young people and have the best wishes of the community for a long and happy life.
Kay and James Fernald have returned home after visiting their grandfather, Oscar Bragg, in Albion the past two weeks.

TROY
Organization Of Troy Fire Co. Is Completed
[Photo caption: FREDERIC J. BAGLEY / Fire Chief]
TROY, April 3—(Special)—The organization of the Troy Volunteer Fire Company was completed Friday evening when the board of directors, meeting at the Home of Orman Gray, elected Frederic J. Bagley as chief. Bagley is also the town fire warden.
The directors voted to purchase a forestry pump and 1000 feet of hose to go with it. The department starts off with a fund of $1500, voted to it at the last town meeting from funds available from the sale of the Lute L. Rogers property, which had been bequeathed to the town.
The company at present numbers 33 members. It has elected Harold Piper, president; Bela Fowler, vice president; Benjamin L. Clark, secretary; Pearl Nutter, treasurer, and as directors, Seavey A. Piper, Walter H. Mitchell, Clyde Fernald, Lyle Fernald, Harold Nutter, Orman C. Gray and Lloyd Myrick.
With the small start in equipment, the members of the fire company hope to be able later to find apparatus to handle heavy fires and a tank truck in which to carry water.
Many of the farms in town have been provided with fire ponds.

Death Notices
BAGLEY, Frederic J., 95, Hampden and Troy; at Hampden, Dec. 12, 2006. Hampden-Gilpatrick Funeral Home, 45 Western Ave., Hampden.

[Obituary notice:]
FREDERIC J. BAGLEY
HAMPDEN and TROY — Frederic J. Bagley, 95, husband of Grace L. Bagley, passed away peacefully Dec. 12, 2006, at his home in Hampden. A complete obituary and service arrangements will be published in Thursday's edition of the Bangor Daily News by the Hampden-Gilpatrick Funeral Home, 45 Western Ave., Hampden.

FREDERIC J. BAGLEY
HAMPDEN and TROY — Frederic J. Bagley, 95, passed away peacefully, Dec. 12, 2006, at his home in Hampden. He was born Aug. 21, 1911, in Troy, at his parent's farm, where he lived for the next 91 years. He was the son of Fred J. and Ethel (Pierce) Bagley.
He graduated from Unity High School in 1929. In 1942, he married Grace Lenentine. For many years, Frederic and Grace ran the family farm in Troy and reared four children. He also worked as a carpenter and served as a substitute mail carrier. Later, he worked in the Unity Post Office. Frederic was very active in local community affairs. He served on the Troy Board of Selectmen for nine years, being first selectman for four years. Frederic was part of the Troy Volunteer Fire Department for most of his life and served as chief for several years. He was a 70 year member and past master of Star in the West Masonic Lodge No. 85 and a member of Josiah H. Drummond No. 91 Order of the Eastern Star, both of Unity. He also served as master and held many other offices in the Seven Star Grange and was a lifetime member of the Troy Union Church.
Frederic is survived by his wife of 64 years, Grace; a daughter, Judith Grace and her husband, Jeffrey Mabee, of Belfast; three sons, Stan and his wife, Terry Burke, of Bangor, Rick and his wife, Karen, of Hampden, and Keith of Portland; five grandchildren, two great-grandchildren; a sister, Betty Hilton of Litchfield; a brother, Wendell of Hudson, Fla.; many nieces and nephews. He was predeceased by a sister, Ruth Myrick and two brothers, Lawrence and Kenton.
A memorial service will be held 11 a.m. Saturday, Dec. 16, at the Troy Union Church with a gathering after at the Troy Grange Hall. Interment will be in Rogers Cemetery, Troy. In lieu of flowers, contributions may be made to the Troy Union Church, care of Marion Paton, 251 Myrick Road, Troy, ME 04987 or to Star in the West Lodge No. 85, P.O. Box 577, Unity, ME 04988. Arrangements entrusted to Hampden-Gilpatrick Funeral Home, 45 Western Ave., Hampden. Messages of condolence or memories may be shared through www.hampdengilpatrick.com

DIED.
In Troy, Nov. 14, Mr. Jonathan Fernald, aged 58 years.
